Predictive Modeling of Forest Biomass for Carbon Management

Carene Kouassi

Due 4pm Friday, May 15, 2026 on Moodle

Load necessary libraries

library(tidyverse)

1. DATA PREPARATION

# Load data (replace with your actual file path)
data <- read_csv("ProjectData_SP2026.csv")
## Rows: 8572 Columns: 25
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr  (1): SECTION
## dbl (24): tcc16, elev, eastness, northness, tri, tpi, ppt, tmean, tmax, tmin...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
# We convert it to a factor for classification
data$CARBON_bin <- factor(ifelse(data$CARBON > 0, "Presence", "Absence"), 
                          levels = c("Absence", "Presence"))

# Remove all other survey variables 
survey_vars <- c("BA", "VOL.CF", "VOL.BF", "COUNT", "DRYBIO", "CARBON")
model_data <- data %>% select(-one_of(survey_vars))

# Split into Training and Testing based on the 'train' column
train_df <- model_data %>% filter(train == 1) %>% select(-train)
test_df  <- model_data %>% filter(train == 0) %>% select(-train)

2. BASELINE MODEL

tab <- table(train_df$CARBON_bin)
proportions <- prop.table(tab)

table_df <- data.frame(
  Class = names(tab),
  Mean  = as.vector(proportions),
  Count = as.vector(tab)
)

table_df <- table_df[order(table_df$Class, decreasing = TRUE), ]
table_df
##      Class      Mean Count
## 2 Presence 0.8362109  3584
## 1  Absence 0.1637891   702

3. EXPLORATORY DATA ANALYSIS (EDA)

summary(train_df)
##    SECTION              tcc16            elev         eastness       
##  Length:4286        Min.   : 0.00   Min.   : 390   Min.   :-100.000  
##  Class :character   1st Qu.:29.00   1st Qu.: 930   1st Qu.: -74.750  
##  Mode  :character   Median :51.00   Median :1208   Median :  -3.000  
##                     Mean   :48.28   Mean   :1259   Mean   :  -2.417  
##                     3rd Qu.:69.00   3rd Qu.:1552   3rd Qu.:  70.000  
##                     Max.   :92.00   Max.   :2616   Max.   : 100.000  
##    northness             tri               tpi                 ppt        
##  Min.   :-100.000   Min.   :  0.125   Min.   :-89.37500   Min.   : 256.0  
##  1st Qu.: -70.000   1st Qu.: 10.875   1st Qu.: -4.00000   1st Qu.: 580.2  
##  Median :  -3.000   Median : 19.250   Median :  0.00000   Median : 825.0

##  3rd Qu.:  65.000   3rd Qu.: 29.500   3rd Qu.:  4.37500   3rd Qu.:1145.0  
##  Max.   : 100.000   Max.   :143.875   Max.   : 78.50000   Max.   :2604.0  
##      tmean             tmax           tmin              tmin01       
##  Min.   :-145.0   Min.   : 335   Min.   :-625.000   Min.   :-1238.0  
##  1st Qu.: 431.0   1st Qu.: 970   1st Qu.:-132.000   1st Qu.: -893.0  
##  Median : 585.0   Median :1164   Median :  14.000   Median : -726.0  
##  Mean   : 561.8   Mean   :1133   Mean   :  -9.107   Mean   : -744.8  
##  3rd Qu.: 712.8   3rd Qu.:1328   3rd Qu.: 122.000   3rd Qu.: -601.0  
##  Max.   :1073.0   Max.   :1688   Max.   : 459.000   Max.   : -283.0  
##      vpdmax           tdmean             def             tnt        
##  Min.   : 447.0   Min.   :-785.00   Min.   : 40.0   Min.   :0.0000  
##  1st Qu.: 865.2   1st Qu.:-195.00   1st Qu.:270.0   1st Qu.:1.0000  
##  Median :1060.0   Median : -47.00   Median :326.0   Median :1.0000  
##  Mean   :1033.0   Mean   : -81.25   Mean   :340.4   Mean   :0.8136  
##  3rd Qu.:1208.8   3rd Qu.:  65.00   3rd Qu.:393.0   3rd Qu.:1.0000  
##  Max.   :1632.0   Max.   : 308.00   Max.   :789.0   Max.   :1.0000  
##       evi              ndvi          CARBON_bin  
##  Min.   :-32768   Min.   :-9890   Absence : 702  
##  1st Qu.: 15056   1st Qu.: 6171   Presence:3584  
##  Median : 19512   Median : 7453                  
##  Mean   : 17979   Mean   : 6884                  
##  3rd Qu.: 22018   3rd Qu.: 8095                  
##  Max.   : 32767   Max.   : 9162
# Correlation Matrix
numeric_vars <- train_df[sapply(train_df, is.numeric)]
numeric_vars <- numeric_vars[, !names(numeric_vars) %in% c("SECTION", "CARBON_bin")]
cor_matrix <- cor(numeric_vars, use = "complete.obs")
corrplot::corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", tl.cex = 0.6)

# Boxplot
boxplot(ndvi ~ CARBON_bin, data = train_df)

boxplot(evi ~ CARBON_bin, data = train_df)

boxplot(tcc16 ~ CARBON_bin, data = train_df)

4. METHOD 1: LASSO (Regularized Logistic Regression)

# Prepare matrix for glmnet (requires matrix input, handles dummy coding)
x_train <- model.matrix(CARBON_bin ~ ., train_df)[,-1]
y_train <- train_df$CARBON_bin

# Cross-validation to find optimal lambda
lasso_cv <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, family = "binomial", alpha = 1)
plot(lasso_cv)

# Predict on test data
x_test <- model.matrix(CARBON_bin ~ ., test_df)[,-1]
lasso_probs <- predict(lasso_cv, newx = x_test, s = "lambda.1se", type = "response")
lasso_pred <- factor(ifelse(lasso_probs > 0.5, "Presence", "Absence"), levels = c("Absence", "Presence"))

# Check coefficients
coef(lasso_cv, s = "lambda.1se")
## 21 x 1 sparse Matrix of class "dgCMatrix"
##                 lambda.1se
## (Intercept)  -1.780148e+00
## SECTIONM333B  .           
## SECTIONM333C  .           
## SECTIONM333D  .           
## tcc16         6.594630e-02
## elev          .           
## eastness      .           
## northness     .           
## tri           4.434059e-03
## tpi           .           
## ppt           .           
## tmean         .           
## tmax          .           
## tmin          .           
## tmin01        .           
## vpdmax        .           
## tdmean        .           
## def           .           
## tnt           7.777147e-01
## evi           .           
## ndvi          7.961747e-05

5. METHOD 2: SUPPORT VECTOR MACHINE (SVM)

# The tune() function helps us find the best parameters (cost and gamma)
# using cross-validation on the training set.
set.seed(123)
svm_tune <- tune(svm, CARBON_bin ~ ., data = train_df, 
                 kernel = "radial",
                 ranges = list(cost = c(0.1, 1, 10), 
                               gamma = c(0.01, 0.1, 1)))

print(svm_tune$best.parameters)
##   cost gamma
## 3   10  0.01
# Extract the best model
best_svm <- svm_tune$best.model

# Predict on the test data
svm_pred <- predict(best_svm, test_df)

table(Predicted = svm_pred, Actual = test_df$CARBON_bin)
##           Actual
## Predicted  Absence Presence
##   Absence      432      107
##   Presence     287     3460

6. METHOD 3: RANDOM FOREST (Non-Linear Ensemble)

# Standard Random Forest (different from a single tree)
rf_model <- randomForest(CARBON_bin ~ ., data = train_df, ntree = 500, importance = TRUE)

# Variable Importance Plot
varImpPlot(rf_model)

# Predict on test data
rf_pred <- predict(rf_model, test_df)

7. PERFORMANCE COMPARISON

# Create Confusion Matrices to get Accuracy, Sensitivity, Specificity
lasso_results <- confusionMatrix(lasso_pred, test_df$CARBON_bin)
svm_results  <- confusionMatrix(svm_pred, test_df$CARBON_bin)
rf_results    <- confusionMatrix(rf_pred, test_df$CARBON_bin)
# Printing Final Accuracy Comparison
cat("LASSO Test Accuracy:", lasso_results$overall['Accuracy'], "\n")
## LASSO Test Accuracy: 0.8978068
cat("SVM Test Accuracy:", svm_results$overall['Accuracy'], "\n")
## SVM Test Accuracy: 0.9080728
cat("Random Forest Test Accuracy:", rf_results$overall['Accuracy'], "\n")
## Random Forest Test Accuracy: 0.9134391
